inflation.title: Inflation (CPI)
inflation.body: Annual inflation rate. Appreciation and alternative return rates are nominal (e.g. 2% appreciation + 2% inflation = 0% real gain). Used to display real-terms indicators: real IRR (Fisher) and constant-euro wealth in KPIs and charts.

displayReal.title: Nominal / real display
displayReal.body: When on: charts (Comparison and Resale) are shown in constant euros, deflated by inflation. In KPI tables, emphasis moves to the real rows. No effect on the calculation engine (always nominal). No-op when inflation = 0.

regime.title: Rental tax regime
regime.body:
LMNP Real: deductible depreciation (property + works).
Micro-BIC: 50% flat deduction.
Bare rental: actual expenses, no depreciation.

tauxActu.title: Discount rate (NPV)
tauxActu.body: Rate used to calculate Net Present Value. Represents the opportunity cost of capital.

rendAlt.title: ETF / alternative investment return (nominal, after-tax)
rendAlt.body: Nominal annual return after tax. Enter after deducting taxation: ~8% gross ETF World → ~5-6% net after 30% flat tax. With 2% inflation, 6% nominal = ~4% real — the real IRR is computed automatically in the KPIs table.

budgetMensuel.title: Monthly housing + investment budget
budgetMensuel.body: Fixed monthly envelope. The surplus not consumed by real costs is automatically invested in ETF.

revalBudget.title: Annual budget growth %/yr
revalBudget.body: Annual increase of your available budget (salary raises). Grows the ETF surplus each year. 0% = fixed budget.

revalCharges.title: Fixed charges annual growth %/yr
revalCharges.body: Annual increase rate for property tax, co-ownership charges, insurance and maintenance reserves. Typically 2–3%/yr in France. Management fees (proportional to rent) are not affected.

investirSurplus.title: Invest surplus in ETF
investirSurplus.body: If enabled: the difference between the monthly budget and real outflows is invested in ETF. The dashed line = pure ETF reference scenario.

apportETF.title: Pure ETF reference contribution
apportETF.body: Starting capital for the pure ETF scenario. No property purchase — contribution + monthly surplus are compounded in ETF.

prixAchat.title: Net purchase price
prixAchat.body: Price paid to the seller, excluding notary and agency fees.

fraisNotaire.title: Notary fees
fraisNotaire.body: 7-8% for existing properties, 2-3% for new builds. Auto: 8% × purchase price.

travaux.title: Renovation works
travaux.body: Under LMNP Real, depreciable over several years.

fraisAgence.title: Buyer's agent fees
fraisAgence.body: Agent commission paid by the buyer.

fraisDossier.title: File & broker fees
fraisDossier.body: Bank origination fees and/or mortgage broker commission. Included in total acquisition cost and borrowed amount.
fraisDossier.code: ct = purchasePrice + notaryFees + works + agencyFees + fileFees

apport.title: Down payment
apport.body: Own capital invested. Reduces the loan amount but also the leverage.

taux.title: Nominal interest rate
taux.body: Nominal loan rate. Auto mode: France average rates (updated 05/2026) — 7y 3.25% · 10y 3.30% · 15y 3.45% · 20y 3.55% · 25y 3.70%.

duree.title: Loan duration
duree.body: Longer = lower monthly payment but higher total cost.

revalBien.title: Property appreciation
revalBien.body: Annual increase assumption. ~2-3%/yr historical average in France.

fraisVente.title: Selling agent fees
fraisVente.body: 3-7% of sale price. Negotiable or avoidable via private sale.

loyer.title: Gross monthly rent
loyer.body: Excluding recoverable charges. Key driver of profitability.

vacance.title: Vacancy rate
vacance.body: 5-8% in tight markets, 10-15% in rural areas.

taxeFonciere.title: Property tax
taxeFonciere.body: Annual local tax. Varies significantly by municipality. Auto: 0.5% × purchase price.

fraisGestion.title: Property management
fraisGestion.body: 6-9% incl. tax of collected rents if using an agency.

provision.title: Maintenance reserve
provision.body: Precautionary savings. Rule of thumb: ~1% of value/yr. Auto: 0.5% × purchase price.

revalLoyer.title: Rent increase
revalLoyer.body: Capped by the rent index. 1-4%/yr depending on inflation.

impotPV.title: Capital gains tax
impotPV.body: 19% income tax + 17.2% social contributions = 36.2%. Progressive allowances. Primary residence exempt.

loyerPerso.title: Current rent you pay
loyerPerso.body: Monthly rent you pay. Rental mode: subtracted from CF. Primary: savings if you buy.
loyerPerso.code:
Real CF (rental) = Rental CF − Personal rent
Savings (primary) = Personal rent − Monthly payment

revalLoyerPerso.title: Market rent revaluation %/yr
revalLoyerPerso.body: Annual increase of your rent. ~1-3%/yr indexed.

taxeFonciereRP.title: Property tax (primary residence)
taxeFonciereRP.body: Same tax as for rental. No housing tax since 2023 for primary residences. Auto: 0.5% × purchase price.

provisionRP.title: Maintenance reserve (primary)
provisionRP.body: Annual maintenance budget. ~1% of property value. Auto: 0.5% × purchase price.

kpi.coutTotal.title: Total acquisition cost
kpi.coutTotal.body: Purchase price + notary fees + works + agency fees. This is the base used to compute yields.

kpi.emprunte.title: Loan amount
kpi.emprunte.body: Total cost − down payment. Financial leverage amplifies gains and losses.

kpi.mensualiteAss.title: Monthly payment + insurance
kpi.mensualiteAss.body: Fixed annuity + mortgage insurance calculated on the initial capital (fixed for the entire loan term).

kpi.coutCredit.title: Total credit cost
kpi.coutCredit.body: Sum of all interest payments + all insurance premiums over the full loan term.

kpi.rendBrut.title: Gross rental yield
kpi.rendBrut.body: Static indicator computed at year 0, before expenses, taxes, and debt service.
kpi.rendBrut.code: Gross yield = Rent×12 / Total acquisition cost

kpi.rendNet.title: Net rental yield
kpi.rendNet.body: Effective rent (after vacancy) − annual expenses, divided by total cost. Excludes debt service and taxes.
kpi.rendNet.code: Net yield = (Eff. rent − Expenses) / Total cost

kpi.cfMensuel.title: Real cash-flow / month
kpi.cfMensuel.body: Net monthly flow for year 1: rents collected − all expenses − loan payment − tax − personal rent. Negative = cash effort.

kpi.effortRP.title: Monthly effort
kpi.effortRP.body: Extra monthly cost of the investment vs staying a renter. Positive = you spend more than today. Negative = investment is cheaper.

kpi.breakeven.title: Cash-flow break-even
kpi.breakeven.body: First year where cumulative net cash flows turn positive. Excludes property appreciation.

kpi.tri.title: IRR — Internal Rate of Return
kpi.tri.body: Discount rate that makes the NPV equal to zero. Accounts for sale proceeds, all net flows, and rent saved/paid depending on mode.

kpi.van.title: NPV — Net Present Value
kpi.van.body: Sum of future cash flows discounted at the opportunity rate. Positive = investment creates value vs the alternative placement.

kpi.moic.title: MOIC — Multiple on Invested Capital
kpi.moic.body: How many times the down payment has been multiplied at the horizon. Not discounted (unlike NPV).
kpi.moic.code: MOIC = (Net sale + Cumul. flows) / Down payment

kpi.coc.title: Cash-on-cash return (yr 1)
kpi.coc.body: Annual yield of the year-1 net operating cash flow relative to the initial down payment. Simple and immediate: how much cash the property generates per euro invested this year. Does not account for resale value or time value of money.

kpi.beRevente.title: Resale break-even
kpi.beRevente.body: First year where reselling no longer makes you lose money, in nominal terms and on the IRR/NPV basis (saved rent for RP / paid rent for LOC reintegrated). Accounts for purchase and sale fees. Ignores opportunity cost (an ETF may do better even if you 'don't lose').
kpi.beRevente.code: first year where reventeNet + Σ(cfN + rent) − downPmt ≥ 0

kpi.bilanRevente.title: Net sale profit
kpi.bilanRevente.body: Cash gain if you sell at this horizon: net sale proceeds + cumulative cash flows − initial down payment. Measures what you actually pocketed in cash.

kpi.bilanTotal.title: Total sale profit
kpi.bilanTotal.body: Overall gain if you sell: net sale proceeds + ETF pocket from reinvested surplus − initial down payment. The most complete metric for comparing two scenarios.

kpi.patNet.title: Net property wealth
kpi.patNet.body: Property equity (estimated value − outstanding capital) + cumulative cash flows − initial down payment.

kpi.patTotal.title: Total wealth
kpi.patTotal.body: Property equity + ETF pocket built from reinvested monthly surplus. Compared to pure ETF scenario to evaluate the crossover.

kpi.etfPur.title: Pure ETF (reference scenario)
kpi.etfPur.body: Alternative scenario: ETF contribution invested upfront + monthly surplus (budget − personal rent) compounded at the alternative return. No property purchase.

kpi.avantageEtf.title: Advantage vs pure ETF
kpi.avantageEtf.body: Total property wealth − pure ETF at the horizon. Positive = real estate outperforms the ETF reference portfolio.

kpi.crossover.title: Crossover vs pure ETF
kpi.crossover.body: First year where total property wealth (equity + ETF from surplus) exceeds the pure ETF. Before this point, the ETF scenario is more profitable.

kpi.triReal.title: Real IRR (inflation-adjusted)
kpi.triReal.body: Nominal IRR adjusted for inflation using the Fisher equation. Measures return in constant euros, above inflation.

kpi.patReal.title: Real wealth (constant euros)
kpi.patReal.body: Nominal wealth deflated by inflation. Expresses purchasing power in today's euros.

cf1.title: Cumulative real estate cash-flow — 30 yrs
cf1.desc: Net cumulative flows from the property only. Rental: rents − expenses − mortgage − taxes − personal rent. Primary: rent saved − expenses − mortgage.

pat.title: Total wealth — 30 yrs
pat.desc: Property equity + ETF portfolio built from monthly surplus. Dark dashed = nominal pure ETF. Light dashed = pure ETF in constant euros.
pat.realNote: Nominal euros · light dashed = deflated pure ETF ({{inflation}}%/yr inflation)

cf3.title: Net annual cash-flow (after tax)
cf3.desc: The jump at loan end is normal: monthly payments stop.

vb.title: Property value over time
vb.desc: Estimated nominal market value based on the configured appreciation rate.
vb.realNote: Nominal values · real gain ≈ appreciation rate − {{inflation}}%/yr inflation

revente.title: Net profit by sale year
revente.desc: Net gain if you sell this year. Primary residence exempt from capital gains. Dashed = pure ETF reference.

reventeCash.title: How many years before you stop losing money?
reventeCash.desc: Cash balance if you resell this year, on the same basis as IRR/NPV: net resale + cumulative flows + saved rent (RP) or − paid rent (LOC) − down payment. The zero crossing (vertical marker) = minimum holding period to avoid a loss. Nominal values, excluding opportunity cost (vs ETF). bilanCash = reventeNet + Σ(cfN + rent) − down payment

amort.title: Annual loan breakdown
amort.desc: At the start most goes to interest, then the capital portion increases progressively.

kpisTable.title: Comparison table — Rental regime:
kpisTable.desc: Total wealth = property equity + ETF from surplus. Pure ETF = contribution + full budget invested without buying.
kpisTable.descReal: Greyed rows = values in constant euros (inflation-adjusted at {{inflation}}%).

auto.tooltipOn: Auto mode active — click to override manually
auto.tooltipOff: Auto mode off — click to enable
auto.zeroGuard: Computed value is 0 — check the purchase price
auto.hint: Fields marked A are auto-calculated. Click A to switch to manual.
